frameworks.name: multiplicative attention-value model
frameworks.one_line: Attention causally biases choice via multiplicative interaction with subjective value

frameworks.name: Bayesian inference over latent values
frameworks.one_line: Gaze allocation arises from inference about latent option values

frameworks.name: post-decisional attention account
frameworks.one_line: Attention reflects choices after the decision has completed

core_question: Does attention causally bias preferential choice through a multiplicative interaction with value, or does it reflect a post-decisional process?
key_assumptions: Multiplicative models predict last fixation is more informative when overall value is high
Multiplicative models predict more attention to chosen option on preference-conflicting trials
Food-choice tasks index value-based decision processes
manipulations: Compared trials by overall value of alternatives
Compared choices conflicting vs consistent with stated preferences
key_findings: No evidence that last fixation was more informative when overall value was high
No evidence of greater attention to chosen option on preference-conflicting trials
A post-decisional attention model explains last-fixation bias and gaze-cascade effect
Post-decisional model captures overall-value effects on response times
interpretations: Multiplicative attention-value and recent normative gaze models are inconsistent with the data
Attention reflects choices after the decision has completed rather than driving them
